name: AaronZhang
cooked:
I’ve been running a small set of experiments to make the differences between three Tapscript constructions more observable.

Pattern Binding target Replayable?
OP_CHECKSIGFROMSTACK Message (stack-supplied) Yes
OP_INTERNALKEY + OP_CHECKSIGFROMSTACK Identity (internal key) Yes, same key
OP_CHECKSIG Transaction sighash No

A tentative framing: IK+CSFS changes where authorization is sourced from, but not what the signature commits to — same opcode family, different binding surface.

Repo (offline harness + Signet anchors + checked-in outputs):

github.com

GitHub - aaron-recompile/bitcoin-signature-binding: Tapscript Schnorr “what verifies against”...

Tapscript Schnorr “what verifies against” experiments — CSFS, IK+CSFS, CHECKSIG — with checked-in outputs.

Does this framing match how others think about it?
topic_title: What exactly is bound in CSFS, IK+CSFS, and CHECKSIG?
topic_html_title: What exactly is bound in CSFS, IK+CSFS, and CHECKSIG?
raw:
I’ve been running a small set of experiments to make the
differences between three Tapscript constructions more
observable.

| Pattern | Binding target | Replayable? |
|----|----|----|
| `OP_CHECKSIGFROMSTACK` | Message (stack-supplied) | Yes |
| `OP_INTERNALKEY` + `OP_CHECKSIGFROMSTACK` | Identity (internal key) | Yes, same key |
| `OP_CHECKSIG` | Transaction sighash | No |

A tentative framing: IK+CSFS changes *where authorization
is sourced from*, but not what the signature commits to —
same opcode family, different binding surface.

Repo (offline harness + Signet anchors + checked-in outputs):

https://github.com/aaron-recompile/bitcoin-signature-binding

Does this framing match how others think about it?
